content_en:
Al-Mutallib ibn ‘Abdullah reported that on one occasion, ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar was standing at ‘Arafaat. He looked at the sun until it descended - like a shield - toward its setting. He # cried, and then his crying intensified. A man beside him said, “O Abu ‘AbdurRahmaan, you have stood beside me many times without doing the same?” He # said, “O people, there does not remain from your world when compared to what has passed from it except what remains from this day of yours compared to what has passed from it.” (Ahmad) Ahmad alone related this hadith.

Another Chain From Ibn ‘Umar #

192 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

of the Hour is a matter that Allah 4 alone possesses. We will further establish this in the section that follows this one, Insia Allah 3. We place our trust in Allah, and upon Him do we depend.

The Prophet 2 Foretold That Those Of His Generation Would Expire Before The Passage Of 100 Years

The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah

Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah s reported that, one month before his death, the Messenger of Allah 2% was asked about the Hour. He #% said, “You ask me about the Hour, when indeed its knowledge is with Allah % only. And by the One Who has my soul in His Hand, I do not know a soul today upon whom 100 years will come.” (Ahmad) Ahmad alone related this narration, and its chain is Hasan, good, its

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 193

narrators are all trustworthy ones.

Another Chain From Jaabir 2

About The Nearness Of The End

‘Aaisha “& said, “When the Bedouins would come to the Messenger of Allah #¢, they would ask him about the Hour. He would look at the youngest person among them and say, ‘If this one lives, old age will not reach him until the hour of each one of you arrives.” (Muslim, who alone related it.)

Anas 4 reported that a man asked the Messenger of Allah 2 “When will the Hour arrive?” A boy from the Ansaar, called Muhammad, was there, and the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “If this young boy lives, then perhaps old age will not reach him until the Hour arrives.” (Muslim)

And in another narration, Anas & said, “A boy, who was of my age, and who belonged to Mugheerah ibn Sho’bah, passed by. The Prophet ## then said, ‘If life for this one is prolonged, then old age will not reach him until the Hour arrives.” (Muslim) Bukhaaree related it through a different chain of narrators.

These narrations point to the frequency of the same question being asked, as well as to its answer. The intended meaning is not an indication of the exact time of the Greater Hour (i.e. the end of this world) based on the old age of the said children in the above narrations; rather, the intended meaning is that the Hour of those

194 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

alive at that time was to arrive before the passage of the stated term, and this is referring to the passing away of that generation. The longest period that people alive at that time were to live for was the time it would take for that young boy to reach old age. These narrations are similar in purport to the previously mentioned hadith: “You ask me about the Hour, when its knowledge is with Allah only. And I swear by Allah that there is not a breathing soul today upon whom. 100 years will come.” And this meaning is also supported by the hadith of ‘Aaishah : “Your Hour will arrive upon you,” This is because when one dies, we rule that he has entered into the Hereafter. The world of the Barzakh, though it is close to this world, is close to the Hereafter and more similar to it than it is to this world. When the appointed period of this world ends, then Allah 3 will order for the Hour to come. The first ones and the last ones will be gathered at an appointed day, and we will further clarify this from the Qur’an and the Sunnah. And we seek help in Allah 8%.

Same Verses About The Resurraction Day

“The Event (the Hour or the punishment of disbelievers and polytheists or the Islamic laws or commandments), ordained by Allah will come to pass, so seek not to hasten it’?

“People ask you concerning the Hour, say: “The knowledge of it is with Allah only. What do you know? Tt may be that the hour is near!’

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 195

“A questioner asked concerning a torment about to befall upon the disbelievers, which none can avert, from Allah, the Lord of the ways of ascent, The angels and the Ruh (Jibreel (Gabriel) ascend to Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years, so be patient (O Muhammad #3), — with a good patience. Verily! They see it (the torment) afar off, but we see it (quite) near. The Day that the sky will be like the boiling filth of oil, (or molten copper or silver or lead, etc.). And the mountains will be like flakes of wool, and no friend will ask of a friend, though they shall be made to see one another ((i.e. on the Day of Resurrection), there will be none but see his father, children and relatives, but he will neither speak to them nor will ask them for any help).

“And the Day when He shall gather (resurrect) them together, (it will be) as if they had not stayed (in the life of this world and graves, etc.) but an hour of a day. They will recognise each other, Ruined indeed will be fhose who denied the meeting with Allah, and were not guided.’

196 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

“It is Allah Who has sent down the Book (the Qur’an) in truth, and the Balance (i.e. to act justly). And what can make you know that perhaps the Hour is close at hand? Those who believe not therein seek to hasten it, while those who believe are fearful of it, and know that it is the very truth. Verily, those who dispute concerning the Hour are ceranly in Peron a an
content_id:
Al-Mutallib ibn 'Abdullah meriwayatkan bahwa pada suatu kesempatan, 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar sedang berdiri di 'Arafaat. Ia memandangi matahari hingga matahari itu turun — seperti perisai — menuju terbenamnya. Ia # menangis, kemudian tangisannya semakin keras. Seorang laki-laki di sebelahnya berkata, "Wahai Abu 'AbdurRahman, engkau telah berdiri di sisiku berkali-kali tanpa melakukan hal yang sama?" Ia # berkata, "Wahai manusia, tidak tersisa dari duniamu dibandingkan dengan apa yang telah berlalu darinya kecuali seperti apa yang tersisa dari harimu ini dibandingkan dengan apa yang telah berlalu darinya." (Ahmad) Hanya Ahmad yang meriwayatkan hadith ini.

Sanad Lain dari Ibn 'Umar #

192 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

tentang Hari Kiamat adalah perkara yang hanya Allah 4 yang memilikinya. Kami akan memperkuat hal ini lebih lanjut pada bagian yang mengikuti ini, Insya Allah 3. Kami bertawakkul kepada Allah, dan kepada-Nya kami bergantung.

Nabi 2 Meramalkan Bahwa Orang-Orang dari Generasinya Akan Wafat Sebelum Berlalunya 100 Tahun

Riwayat Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah

Jaabir ibn 'Abdullah s meriwayatkan bahwa, sebulan sebelum wafatnya, Rasulullah 2% ditanya tentang Hari Kiamat. Beliau #% bersabda, "Kalian bertanya kepadaku tentang Hari Kiamat, padahal sesungguhnya ilmunya hanya ada pada Allah % semata. Dan demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, aku tidak mengetahui seorang jiwa pun hari ini yang akan hidup hingga 100 tahun." (Ahmad) Hanya Ahmad yang meriwayatkan riwayat ini, dan sanadnya adalah Hasan, baik, para

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 193

perawinya semuanya adalah orang-orang yang terpercaya.

Sanad Lain dari Jaabir 2

Tentang Dekatnya Hari Akhir

'Aisyah "& berkata, "Ketika orang-orang Badui datang kepada Rasulullah #¢, mereka bertanya kepada beliau tentang Hari Kiamat. Beliau memandang orang yang paling muda di antara mereka dan berkata, 'Jika orang ini hidup, maka usia tua tidak akan mendatanginya hingga Hari Kiamat masing-masing dari kalian tiba.'" (Muslim, yang sendirian meriwayatkannya.)

Anas 4 meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Rasulullah 2, "Kapan Hari Kiamat akan tiba?" Seorang anak laki-laki dari kaum Anshar, bernama Muhammad, ada di sana, dan Rasulullah 2% bersabda, "Jika anak muda ini hidup, maka mungkin usia tua tidak akan mendatanginya hingga Hari Kiamat tiba." (Muslim)

Dan dalam riwayat lain, Anas & berkata, "Seorang anak laki-laki, yang seusia denganku, dan yang termasuk dalam keluarga Mughirah ibn Syu'bah, lewat. Nabi ## kemudian bersabda, 'Jika kehidupan orang ini dipanjangkan, maka usia tua tidak akan mendatanginya hingga Hari Kiamat tiba.'" (Muslim) Bukhari meriwayatkannya melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Riwayat-riwayat ini menunjukkan seringnya pertanyaan yang sama diajukan, serta jawabannya. Makna yang dimaksud bukanlah indikasi waktu yang tepat dari Hari Kiamat Besar (yakni, akhir dunia ini) berdasarkan usia tua anak-anak yang disebutkan dalam riwayat-riwayat di atas; melainkan, makna yang dimaksud adalah bahwa Hari Kiamat bagi mereka yang

194 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

hidup pada saat itu akan tiba sebelum berlalunya masa yang disebutkan, dan ini merujuk pada wafatnya generasi tersebut. Masa terpanjang yang akan dijalani oleh orang-orang yang hidup pada saat itu adalah waktu yang diperlukan oleh anak muda itu untuk mencapai usia tua. Riwayat-riwayat ini serupa maknanya dengan hadith yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya: "Kalian bertanya kepadaku tentang Hari Kiamat, padahal ilmunya hanya ada pada Allah. Dan aku bersumpah demi Allah bahwa tidak ada seorang jiwa pun yang bernapas hari ini yang akan hidup hingga 100 tahun." Dan makna ini juga didukung oleh hadith 'Aisyah: "Hari Kiamatmu akan mendatangimu." Ini karena ketika seseorang meninggal, kita menghukumi bahwa ia telah masuk ke Akhirat. Alam Barzakh, meskipun dekat dengan dunia ini, lebih dekat dengan Akhirat dan lebih menyerupainya daripada menyerupai dunia ini. Ketika masa yang telah ditetapkan bagi dunia ini berakhir, maka Allah 3 akan memerintahkan agar Hari Kiamat tiba. Orang-orang terdahulu dan orang-orang terakhir akan dikumpulkan pada suatu hari yang telah ditetapkan, dan kami akan menjelaskan hal ini lebih lanjut dari Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah. Dan kami memohon pertolongan kepada Allah 8%.

Ayat-Ayat yang Sama Tentang Hari Kebangkitan

oh 7g he otk ty Cotte M6225 DAE 8 pA alice Ed Gil "Telah dekat bagi manusia perhitungan mereka, sementara mereka berpaling dalam kelalaian.")

"Peristiwa (Hari Kiamat atau azab bagi orang-orang kafir dan musyrik atau hukum-hukum Islam atau perintah-perintah-Nya), yang telah ditetapkan oleh Allah pasti akan terjadi, maka janganlah meminta untuk mempercepat kedatangannya"

"Manusia bertanya kepadamu tentang Hari Kiamat, katakanlah: 'Ilmunya hanya ada pada Allah. Apa yang engkau ketahui? Mungkin saja Hari Kiamat itu sudah dekat!'"

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 195

eal yo Bb A EGS o ay yi OC Ud eee a bos cans Meaich v0 cdl OOF B50 te By Mo Le he pho KH Soh et ES 0 oil S50 Bie TR SS

"Seorang penanya bertanya tentang azab yang akan menimpa orang-orang kafir, yang tidak dapat dielakkan oleh siapa pun, dari Allah, Tuhan pemilik tangga-tangga naik. Para malaikat dan Ruh (Jibril (Gabriel)) naik kepada-Nya dalam sehari yang ukurannya lima puluh ribu tahun, maka bersabarlah (wahai Muhammad #3) — dengan kesabaran yang baik. Sesungguhnya! Mereka melihatnya (azab itu) dari jauh, tetapi kami melihatnya (sangat) dekat. Pada hari ketika langit akan seperti endapan minyak yang mendidih, (atau tembaga, perak, atau timah cair, dan sebagainya). Dan gunung-gunung akan seperti serpihan wol, dan tidak ada seorang sahabat pun yang akan menanyakan keadaan sahabatnya, meskipun mereka akan diperlihatkan satu sama lain ((yakni, pada Hari Kebangkitan), tidak ada seorang pun kecuali ia melihat ayah, anak-anak, dan kerabatnya, namun ia tidak akan berbicara kepada mereka dan tidak akan meminta pertolongan dari mereka).

"Dan pada hari ketika Dia mengumpulkan (membangkitkan) mereka semua, (seolah-olah) mereka tidak pernah tinggal (dalam kehidupan dunia ini dan di dalam kubur, dan sebagainya) kecuali sesaat saja dari sehari. Mereka akan saling mengenal satu sama lain. Sungguh merugilah orang-orang yang mendustakan pertemuan dengan Allah, dan mereka tidak mendapat petunjuk.'"

196 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Erbil BS 65 Stadty BL CO Sl wll ite er ods & itd i a Me ec OO as

"Allah-lah yang telah menurunkan Kitab (Al-Qur'an) dengan kebenaran, dan Neraca (yakni, untuk berlaku adil). Dan apakah yang membuatmu tahu bahwa mungkin Hari Kiamat sudah dekat? Orang-orang yang tidak beriman padanya ingin mempercepat kedatangannya, sedangkan orang-orang yang beriman merasa takut terhadapnya, dan mengetahui bahwa itu adalah kebenaran yang nyata. Sesungguhnya, orang-orang yang berbantah-bantahan tentang Hari Kiamat pasti dalam kesesatan yang jauh."
content_ar: Al-Mutallib ibn ‘Abdullah reported that on one occasion, ‘Abdullah ibn ‘عمر was standing at ‘Arafaat. He looked at the sun حتى it descended - like a shield - toward its setting. He # cried, and then his crying intensified. A man beside him said, “O Abu ‘AbdurRahmaan, you have stood beside me many times without doing the same?” He # said, “O people, there does not remain from your الدنيا عندما compared to what has passed from it except what remains from this day of yours compared to what has passed from it.” (أحمد) أحمد alone related this الحديث. Another Chain From Ibn ‘عمر # 192 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations of the Hour is a matter that الله 4 alone possesses. We will further establish this in the الفصل that follows this one, Insia الله 3. We place our trust in الله, and upon Him do we depend. النبي ﷺ 2 Foretold That Those Of His Generation Would Expire قبل The Passage Of 100 Years The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah s reported that, one month قبل his الموت, الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% was asked about the Hour. He #% said, “You ask me about the Hour, عندما indeed its العلم is with الله % only. And by the One Who has my النفس in His Hand, I do not know a النفس today upon whom 100 years will come.” (أحمد) أحمد alone related this narration, and its chain is حسن, الخير, its الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 193 narrators are all trustworthy ones. Another Chain From Jaabir 2 About The Nearness Of The End ‘Aaisha “& said, “عندما the Bedouins would come to الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢, they would ask him about the Hour. He would look at the youngest person among them and say, ‘إذا this one lives, old age will not reach him حتى the hour of each one of you arrives.” (مسلم, who alone related it.) أنس 4 reported that a man asked الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 “عندما will the Hour arrive?” A boy from the Ansaar, called محمد ﷺ, was there, and الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “إذا this young boy lives, then perhaps old age will not reach him حتى the Hour arrives.” (مسلم) And in another narration, أنس & said, “A boy, who was of my age, and who belonged to Mugheerah ibn Sho’bah, passed by. النبي ﷺ ## then said, ‘إذا الحياة for this one is prolonged, then old age will not reach him حتى the Hour arrives.” (مسلم) Bukhaaree related it through a different chain of narrators. These narrations point to the frequency of the same question being asked, as well as to its answer. The intended meaning is not an indication of the exact time of the Greater Hour (i.e. the end of this الدنيا) based on the old age of tقال الأولاد in the above narrations; rather, the intended meaning is that the Hour of those 194 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations alive at that time was to arrive قبل the passage of the stated term, and this is referring to the passing away of that generation. The longest period that people alive at that time were to live for was the time it would take for that young boy to reach old age. These narrations are similar in purport to the previously mentioned الحديث: “You ask me about the Hour, عندما its العلم is with الله only. And I swear by الله that there is not a breathing النفس today upon whom. 100 years will come.” And this meaning is أيضاً supported by the الحديث of ‘Aaishah : “Your Hour will arrive upon you,” This is لأن عندما one dies, we rule that he has entered into الآخرة. The الدنيا of the Barzakh, though it is close to this الدنيا, is close to الآخرة and more similar to it than it is to this الدنيا. عندما the appointed period of this الدنيا ends, then الله 3 will order for the Hour to come. The أولاً ones and the last ones will be gathered at an appointed day, and we will further clarify this from the Qur’an and the السنة. And we seek help in الله 8%.
